params.text:
The chain of infection

For an infection to spread, six links must all connect - like a chain. If you break any single link, you stop the infection reaching the next person. This is the idea behind everything you do in IPC.

  • Infectious agent - the germ itself (bacteria, virus, fungus).
  • Reservoir - where the germ lives and multiplies (a person, contaminated equipment, water, food).
  • Portal of exit - how it leaves the reservoir (coughing, a wound, faeces, blood).
  • Mode of transmission - how it travels (unwashed hands, droplets in the air, contaminated surfaces).
  • Portal of entry - how it gets into the next person (mouth, nose, eyes, broken skin, a catheter).
  • Susceptible host - someone whose defences let the germ take hold (frail, unwell, or immunosuppressed).

Your everyday actions target these links. Hand hygiene and cleaning break transmission; covering coughs blocks the portal of exit; PPE and wound care protect the portal of entry; good nutrition and vaccination strengthen the host.

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: Match each link in the chain of infection to what it means and how you break it.

dialogs.answer: The germ that causes disease - a bacterium, virus or fungus. We break this link by killing germs through cleaning, decontamination and antimicrobial stewardship.
The place a germ lives and multiplies - a person, contaminated equipment, standing water or food. We break this link by cleaning and safely managing waste and equipment.
How the germ leaves its reservoir - coughing, sneezing, a draining wound, blood or faeces. We break this link with respiratory hygiene, wound dressings and safe handling of body fluids.
How the germ travels - most often on unwashed hands, but also via droplets, airborne particles or contaminated surfaces. Hand hygiene breaks this link most powerfully.
How the germ enters the next person - mouth, nose, eyes, broken skin, or a device like a urinary catheter. PPE, aseptic technique and covering wounds protect this link.
A person whose defences are lowered by age, illness or immunosuppression. We strengthen the host through vaccination, good nutrition, hydration and skin care.
